---
title: "CMP specification template"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.html >}}

<div class="paper-title">Normative CMP Specification Template</div>

Copy this structure for every shortlisted CMP. Text labelled normative governs
implementation; explanatory evidence belongs in the evidence section.

## Identity and authority

| Field | Required value |
|---|---|
| CMP identifier | Stable identifier; never reused |
| Version | Semantic or dated version |
| Status | Test, shortlisted, recommended, adopted, or retired |
| Effective period | First and last management year, if applicable |
| Decision record | Body, meeting, document, date, and paragraph |
| Code reference | Repository, commit, file, and function/control name |

## Normative inputs

For every input state the owner, series name, unit, spatial coverage, seasonal
coverage, annual cutoff date, revision policy, missing-value rule, and
transformation. Specify exactly which data vintages are eligible.

## Normative calculation

Document, in order:

1. data validation and exclusions;
2. estimator calculation;
3. status metric and reference period;
4. HCR equation and every parameter;
5. interannual constraints;
6. rounding and numerical precision;
7. implementation allocation; and
8. exceptional-circumstances checks.

## Worked example

Provide frozen input data, intermediate values, final advice, and a checksum or
automated regression test. A reviewer must be able to reproduce the published
answer without access to mutable external data.

## Evidence and limitations

Link to MSE runs, operating models, performance metrics, diagnostics, and
known limitations. Evidence explains selection but does not redefine the CMP.

## Change history

Every change must state whether it is editorial, implementation-equivalent, or
scientifically material. Scientifically material changes require renewed MSE
evaluation unless an approving decision explicitly states otherwise.
